text.title: spatial-design-tools Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns repeatedly observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Team spends weeks polishing 3D mockups in Figma before any engine work; production team starts from scratch.
antipattern.root-cause: Design team gravitated to familiar 2D tooling; in-engine prototype skipped.
antipattern.fix: Set hard phase gates: ≤2 weeks concept, then in-engine prototype mandatory.

antipattern.symptom: Same app ships USDZ for visionOS, glTF for Quest, FBX from artist; build pipeline breaks weekly.
antipattern.root-cause: No format standard declared; each artist used their preferred export.
antipattern.fix: Declare per-platform format; document FBX→USD/glTF conversion as a CI step.

antipattern.symptom: Artists deliver 500K-poly hero asset; engineering rejects; one month of rework.
antipattern.root-cause: Budgets not communicated until QA stage.
antipattern.fix: Publish polygon, file-size, draw-call budgets in concept-stage config; CI gate on import.

antipattern.symptom: Team picked Unity because 'that's what we used last time'; project hits Unreal-only feature need late.
antipattern.root-cause: Engine choice was default-driven, not justified.
antipattern.fix: Engine choice in config must include team-skill + platform-reach + license rationale.
